questions.question: 6. Negative sequence currents rotates with respect to field winding at

explanations.a: Incorrect. Positive sequence rotates at synchronous speed relative to stator.
explanations.b: Correct. Negative sequence rotates opposite to rotor direction at synchronous speed, so relative speed is 2× synchronous speed.
explanations.c: Incorrect. This is not correct for negative sequence.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Zero sequence does not produce a rotating field, but negative sequence does.

questions.question: 7. Zero sequence current can flow from a transmission line to a transformer if the windings are

explanations.a: Incorrect. Grounded star on one side and delta on other blocks zero sequence.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Delta/Star without ground blocks zero sequence on delta side.
explanations.c: Correct. Star (source side) with grounded star provides a path for zero sequence currents.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Delta/Delta blocks zero sequence currents completely.

questions.question: 8. In which fault the positive sequence component of voltage at fault point is zero

explanations.a: Correct. In a bolted three-phase fault, the voltage at fault point becomes zero, so positive sequence voltage is zero.
explanations.b: Incorrect. In L-L fault, positive sequence voltage is not zero.
explanations.c: Incorrect. In L-L-G fault, positive sequence voltage is not zero.
explanations.d: Incorrect. In L-G fault, positive sequence voltage is not zero.

questions.question: 9. If I<sub>a1</sub> is the positive sequence current of an alternator and Z<sub>1</sub>, Z<sub>2</sub> & Z<sub>0</sub> are the sequence impedances of the alternator, the voltage drop produced in L-G fault will be

explanations.a: Incorrect. For L-G fault, all three sequence networks are in series.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Zero sequence is also involved.
explanations.c: Correct. For a line-to-ground fault, the fault current If = 3I<sub>a1</sub> and voltage drop = I<sub>a1</sub>(Z<sub>1</sub>+Z<sub>2</sub>+Z<sub>0</sub>).
explanations.d: Incorrect. Positive sequence impedance must be included.

questions.question: 10. Which statement is correct for positive, negative and zero sequence impedances

answers.a: (Z<sub>1</sub> greater than Z<sub>2</sub> greater than Z<sub>0</sub>)
answers.b: (Z<sub>1</sub> less than Z<sub>2</sub> less than Z<sub>0</sub>)
answers.c: (Z<sub>0</sub> less than Z<sub>1</sub> less than Z<sub>2</sub>)

explanations.a: Correct. Typically for synchronous machines: X<sub>d</sub> (positive) > X<sub>2</sub> (negative) > X<sub>0</sub> (zero).
explanations.c: Incorrect. X<sub>0</sub> is usually the smallest.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Option (a) is correct for most synchronous machines.
